Question: Find the most common power supply type per country.
Expected SQL: SELECT country, power_supply, COUNT(*) FROM projects p JOIN sites s ON p.project_id = s.project_id JOIN site_gateways sg ON s.site_id = sg.site_id JOIN gateways g ON sg.gateway_id = g.gateway_id GROUP BY country, power_supply;
Agent SQL:
WITH power_supply_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    p.country,
    g.power_supply,
    COUNT(*) as cnt
  FROM gateways g
  JOIN site_gateways sg ON g.gateway_id = sg.gateway_id
  JOIN sites s ON sg.site_id = s.site_id
  JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.project_id
  WHERE g.power_supply IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.country, g.power_supply
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT 
    country,
    power_supply,
    cnt,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY cnt DESC) as rn
  FROM power_supply_counts
)
SELECT 
  country,
  power_supply,
  cnt
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY country;

Question: Find sequences where value exceeds 50 three times consecutively.
Expected SQL: SELECT * FROM (SELECT value, sensor_id, timestamp, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY sensor_id ORDER BY timestamp) as row_num FROM raw_measurements WHERE value > 50) t WHERE row_num >= 3;
Agent SQL:
WITH ordered_measurements AS (
  SELECT 
    measurement_id,
    variable_id,
    sensor_id,
    value,
    timestamp,
    LAG(value, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY variable_id, sensor_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS prev_value_1,
    LAG(value, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY variable_id, sensor_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS prev_value_2
  FROM raw_measurements
)
SELECT 
  measurement_id,
  variable_id,
  sensor_id,
  value,
  timestamp
FROM ordered_measurements
WHERE value > 50

  AND prev_value_2 > 50
ORDER BY variable_id, sensor_id, timestamp;

Question: Identify the first gateway to upload data each day.
Expected SQL: SELECT DISTINCT ON (DATE(timestamp)) DATE(timestamp), sensor_id FROM raw_measurements ORDER BY DATE(timestamp), timestamp ASC;
Agent SQL:
WITH daily_first_uploads AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE(rm.timestamp) AS upload_date,
    c.gateway_id,
    MIN(rm.timestamp) AS first_upload_time
  FROM raw_measurements rm
  JOIN configs c ON rm.variable_id IN (
    SELECT variable_id 
    FROM raw_measurements 
    WHERE sensor_id IN (
      SELECT sensor_id 
      FROM raw_measurements
    )
  )
  WHERE c.gateway_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY DATE(rm.timestamp), c.gateway_id
),
first_gateway_per_day AS (
  SELECT 
    upload_date,
    gateway_id,
    first_upload_time,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY upload_date ORDER BY first_upload_time) AS rn
  FROM daily_first_uploads
)
SELECT 
  fg.upload_date,
  fg.gateway_id,
  g.name,
  fg.first_upload_time
FROM first_gateway_per_day fg
JOIN gateways g ON fg.gateway_id = g.gateway_id
WHERE fg.rn = 1
ORDER BY fg.upload_date DESC;
Error Note: Column count mismatch & Key column missing! Expected: ['date', 'sensor_id'], Missing: ['date', 'sensor_id']

Question: Day with highest volatility (max - min) per month.
Expected SQL: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', timestamp), DATE(timestamp), MAX(value) - MIN(value) as volatility FROM raw_measurements GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY volatility DESC;
Agent SQL:
WITH daily_volatility AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', timestamp) AS month,
    DATE(timestamp) AS day,
    MAX(value) - MIN(value) AS volatility
  FROM raw_measurements
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', timestamp), DATE(timestamp)
),
ranked_days AS (
  SELECT 
    month,
    day,
    volatility,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY month ORDER BY volatility DESC) AS rn
  FROM daily_volatility
)
SELECT 
  month,
  day,
  volatility
FROM ranked_days
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY month;
